stimulus: Very little is known about the role nocturnal mammals, such as bats, play in cacao tree pollination because it is difficult to track them in dense canopy at night. To address this problem, a team of biologists installed infrared motion-triggered cameras to record visits to cacao flowers throughout the day and night. The footage showed that while most pollinator visits were made by midges, nearly a quarter of visits were made by small fruit bats. Additionally, flowers visited by both bats and midges set more fruit than flowers visited only by midges.
stem: Which choice best states the function of the underlined sentence?

answerOptions.content: To announce an unexpected research finding about cacao trees
To question a claim biologists make about cacao pollinators
To explain why fruit bats prefer cacao flowers to other plants
To describe an approach a team of biologists used to study pollinators

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The underlined sentence describes how a team of biologists addressed the problem of monitoring nocturnal mammals in the canopy: they installed infrared motion-triggered cameras to record visits to cacao flowers around the clock. It therefore describes an approach the team used to study pollinators.

Choice A is incorrect because the underlined sentence describes a method, not a finding, and the text does not indicate that any findings were unexpected. Choice B is incorrect because nothing in the underlined sentence questions a claim about pollinators. Choice C is incorrect because the text does not address whether fruit bats prefer cacao flowers.
